name: Curried Stuffed Acorn Squash
recipeInstructions:
1. Halve the Acorn squash and remove the seeds. In a 13-by-9-inch baking pan, combine apple juice and half of the broth. Place the squash cut-side down in the pan. Bake at 375 degrees for 30 minutes.
2. In a small bowl, stir together chutney and two tablespoons of the broth; set aside.
3. Turn the squash cut-side up. Prick the inside of the squash with a fork. Divide the chutney mixture evenly among the squash halves. Bake for an additional 20 minutes.
4. Meanwhile, prepare the stuffing: In a 2-quart saucepan over medium heat, cook celery in butter until tender and add curry. Add remaining broth and remove from heat.
5. Combine the stuffing and dried fruit in a large bowl. Pour the broth mixture over the stuffing mixture and toss to mix well.
6. Spoon the stuffing mixture into the squash halves, mounding slightly. Cover loosely with foil. Bake for an additional 20 minutes or until the stuffing is hot. Serve with roast lamb or chicken.
7. Makes 6 servings.
